LATEINER IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFT TAKATA CORPORATION AND TK HOLDINGS INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE FOR THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES FILED BY PLAINTIFF CARPOL, RONALD , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH CARPOL GIBLIN, JAMES GIBLIN, CATHERINE CARPOL, SUZANNE CARPOL, NICHOLAS

LAW AND MOTION 301, AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS TK HOLDINGS, INC., TAKATA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE FOR THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS DENIED. UNDOUBTEDLY SOME WITNESSES WILL BE INCONVENIENCED WHEREVER THIS TRIAL IS HELD, BUT DEFENDANT HAS NOT CONVINCED THE COURT THROUGH THE EVIDENCE IT PRESENTS THAT THE INCONVENIENCE OF COMING TO SAN FRANCISCO FOR THE RELATIVELY FEW WITNESSES LOCATED IN NAPA IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY A CHANGE IN VENUE, ESPECIALLY AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE AND CONSIDERING THAT DEFENDANT HAD COLLECTED ITS DECLARATIONS BY APRIL OF THIS YEAR. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: DIANA CHEN CSR # 11312 (301/PJB)

LAW AND MOTION 301, AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC., NISSAN MOTOR CO., LTD'S JOINDER TO TAKATA CORPORATION AND TK HOLDING, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE FOR THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS DENIED. UNDOUBTEDLY SOME WITNESSES WILL BE INCONVENIENCED WHEREVER THIS TRIAL IS HELD, BUT DEFENDANT HAS NOT CONVINCED THE COURT THROUGH THE EVIDENCE IT PRESENTS THAT THE INCONVENIENCE OF COMING TO SAN FRANCISCO FOR THE RELATIVELY FEW WITNESSES LOCATED IN NAPA IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY A CHANGE IN VENUE, ESPECIALLY AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE AND CONSIDERING THAT DEFENDANT HAD COLLECTED ITS DECLARATIONS BY APRIL OF THIS YEAR. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: DIANA CHEN CSR # 11312 (301/PJB)

Ntc Of Joinder To Takata Corporation And Tk Holding, Inc.'S Mtn To Transfer Venue For The Convenience Of Witnesses
Off Calendar Aug-26-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ANDREW T. RYAN, ESQ, (BOWMAN AND BROOKE, LLP), (310)380-6554, FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC., NISSAN MOTOR COMPANY, LTD.
JEREMY N. LATEINER, ESQ. (HINTON, ALFERT & SUMNER), (925)932-6006 AND (415)999-0489, FOR PLAINTIFFS/OPPOSING PARTIES, RONALD CARPOL, individually and as Successor in Interest to the Estate of ELIZABETH CARPOL, JAMES GIBLIN, CATHERINE GIBLIN, SUZANNE CARPOL, NICHOLAS CARPOL

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC., NISSAN MOTOR CO., LTD'S Joinder To Takata Corporation And Tk Holding, Inc.'S MOTION To Transfer Venue For The Convenience Of Witnesses IS DENIED. UNDOUBTEDLY SOME WITNESSES WILL BE INCONVENIENCED WHEREVER THIS TRIAL IS HELD, BUT DEFENDANT HAS NOT CONVINCED THE COURT THROUGH THE EVIDENCE IT PRESENTS THAT THE INCONVENIENCE OF COMING TO SAN FRANCISCO FOR THE RELATIVELY FEW WITNESSES LOCATED IN NAPA IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY A CHANGE IN VENUE, ESPECIALLY AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE AND CONSIDERING THAT DEFENDANT HAD COLLECTED ITS DECLARATIONS BY APRIL OF THIS YEAR. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Diana Chen CSR # 11312
(301/PJB/PM)

UNDOUBTEDLY SOME WITNESSES WILL BE INCONVENIENCED WHEREVER THIS TRIAL IS HELD, BUT DEFENDANT HAS NOT CONVINCED THE COURT THROUGH THE EVIDENCE IT PRESENTS THAT THE INCONVENIENCE OF COMING TO SAN FRANCISCO FOR THE RELATIVELY FEW WITNESSES LOCATED IN NAPA IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY A CHANGE IN VENUE, ESPECIALLY AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE AND CONSIDERING THAT DEFENDANT HAD COLLECTED ITS DECLARATIONS BY APRIL OF THIS YEAR.
